summary: Hardcoded English 'Get Started' CTA on the Japanese route bypasses next-intl and lacks a JA catalog entry, and trust-signal qualifiers ('verified', 'Vault contract') also render in English on /ja. Visual diff is a minor CTA gradient-to-solid change (cosmetic) and performance is within budget. Translator/component owner must add t() wrapper plus ja keys; designer arbitration is not required for the copy fix, but the gradient change should be confirmed against design intent.
must_fix_before_ship: Hardcoded English CTA on /ja ('Get Started') in components/marketing/HomeHero.tsx:47
Untranslated English CTA on Japanese route (a11y impact for JA screen readers)
unresolved_questions: Is the CTA gradient->solid (#C41E3A->#9F0F1A gradient -> flat #C41E3D) an intentional design update or an unintended regression? Needs design sign-off to either accept new baseline or revert.
Primary-CTA-count rule (exactly one bg-gradient-hinomaru above the fold) cannot be verified from a11y tree alone — needs DOM/class-level check.
